message:
feat(fp-io): add read_sql index_col promotion parity (frankenpandas-1v8y)

pandas `pd.read_sql(sql, con, index_col=...)` and
`pd.read_sql_table(table, con, index_col=...)` materialize the
named result column as the DataFrame index. The existing
`read_sql` always built a 0..n RangeIndex, leaving callers to do
the column → index promotion themselves.

Adds:
- `read_sql_with_index_col(conn, query, index_col)` — `Some(name)`
  promotes that column to the row index and removes it from the
  data set; `None` is a noop returning the same shape as
  `read_sql`.
- `read_sql_table_with_index_col(conn, table, index_col)` — same
  semantics over `read_sql_table`.
- `promote_column_to_index(frame, col_name)` — shared helper that
  builds an Index from the column's values (Int64/Utf8/Timedelta
  pass through; bools and floats stringify; NaN → "NaN" Utf8
  marker), drops the source column, and copies the column name into
  the resulting Index.name().

Returns `IoError::Sql` when the named column is absent from the
result set.

Four tests: index_col=Some promotes the column and drops it from
data; index_col=None is a noop relative to baseline read_sql_table;
unknown column rejects; arbitrary SELECT (with reordering and
aliases) still respects the requested index column.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
